free_text: Measurement of the angle-correlated neutron spectrum
for Be-9(N,2N) reaction with a pencil-beam DT neutron
Figures of dif.CS. Total n,2n CS in text.
Pencil-beam DT neutron source at FNS facility.
DT neutron beam of 2 cm in diam. Neutron intensity
10**6 n/cm**2/sec inside the beam. Outside the beam
the BG neutron intensity is suppressed to around
Pulse shape discrimination is applied to exclude
Absolute value of neutron flux was derived by
the foil activation technique with Al foil.
Two spherical NE213 detectors (4cm in diam.) as
neutron detectors. Polyethylene block up to around 10cm
in thickness was placed between two detectors to
The lower measurable neutron energy 1.2 MeV.
U-238 fission chamber,located on the beam line,
behind the sample -to monitor the relative neutron flux
Metallic beryllium of 2 cm in diam., 2 cm length.
Al foil attached on the upstream surface of sample.
-For inter-detector scattering. The rate of the
inter-detector scattering component was estimated to
be about 5 to 40% depending on the scattering angle.
Estimated correction factor increases as the distance
between two detectors becomes closer. It was confirmed
that the component could effectively be suppressed by
incident/emitted neutrons. Correction factor of the
attenuation and multiple-scattering effects was found
value of neutron flux by normalizing the relative
values obtained by the U-238 fission chamber.
Measured raw pulse height spectra were transformed
into light output spectra. The position of Compton edge
formed by the 1.275MeV gamma ray emitted from 22Na was
used for the light unit calibration. The light output
spectra were unfolded using FORIST code. The necessary
response function was calculated with SCINFUL code.
added. BIB information was updated in Subents 001,005.

The statistical errors became too small
because of integration over angle and energy, other
systematic errors became dominant and are taken into
